---
title: "June 25: Decision Trees and Markov Models"
---

## Learning objectives

By the end of Day 1, participants should be able to:

- Explain when to use a decision tree versus a Markov model.
- Build a simple decision tree in R.
- Define health states, cycles, transition probabilities, costs, and utilities.
- Run a cohort Markov model.
- Calculate total costs, QALYs, and incremental cost-effectiveness ratios.

## Suggested structure

| Session | Topic | Materials |
|---|---|---|
| Morning 1 | Introduction to economic evaluation and decision trees | Slides, code demo |
| Morning 2 | Decision tree coding lab | Exercise 1 |
| Afternoon 1 | Markov model concepts | Slides, model template |
| Afternoon 2 | Cohort Markov model coding lab | Exercise 2 |
| Homework | Build and interpret a simple Markov model | Homework 1 |

## Files

- [Day 1 slides placeholder](../slides/day1/day1-slides.pdf)
- [Decision tree code](../code/day1/01-decision-tree.R)
- [Markov model code](../code/day1/02-markov-model.R)
- [Exercise 1](../exercises/exercise-01-decision-tree.qmd)
- [Exercise 2](../exercises/exercise-02-markov-model.qmd)
